entries.text: Military — Resource management: +strategic Production in all cities, +strategic yield.
Military — Third Alternative: ++gold and +culture from military and strategic developments.
Military — Survey: double experience for recon units.
Military — Disipline: +++combat strength when fighting bandits.
Military — Agoge: +Production toward ranged and anti-calvary units.
Military — Maritime industries: ++production towards naval units.
Military — Maneuver = +production towards calvary units.
Military — Conscription: -gold cost for unit maintenance.
Military — Limitanei - +loyalty for cities with garrisons.
Military — Raid: Double yields for pillaging.
Military — Veterancy: +production towards encampements and harbors.
Military — Equestrian orders: +Yield for horses and iron .
Military — Bastions: +city defense and ranged strength.
Military — Limes: double production towards defensive buildings.
Military — Feudal contract: +production towards melee, ranged, and anti-calvary units.
Military — Retainers: +amenity for cities with a garrison.
Military — Sack: +Yield from pillaging districts.
Military — Professional army: -cost toward unit upgrades.
Military — Retinues: -resource cost towards unit upgrades.
Military — Craftsmen: double industrial zone adjacency bonuses.
Military — Chivalry: +production towards light and heavy calvary units.
Military — Native conquest: +gold per earlier era unit conquest.
Military — Wars of religion: +combat strength towards civs with different religion .
Military — Logistics: +movement in friendly territory .
Military — Drill Manuals: +yields for niter and coal.
Military — Grande Armee: +production for melee ranged and anti-calvary units.
Military — National identity: less combat strength loss from taking damage in battle.
Military — Press Gangs: double production towards naval units.
Military — Military research: +science for military buildings.
Military — Force modernization: -gold cost for unit upgrades.
Military — Total war: double Yield from coastal pillaging and raids and for plundering trade routes.
Military — Propaganda: -war weariness over time.
Military — Levee en masse: -gold cost for unit maintenance.
Military — Lightning warfare: +production for calvary units.
Military — Patriotic war: double production for all military support units.
Military — Internation waters: Double production towards all naval units.
Military — Military first: +production on all melee, anti calvary, and ranged units.
Military — Integrated Space cell: +production towards military-beneficial sciences.
Military — Second strike capability: -gold cost for large military weapon maintenance.
Military — Strategic air force: +production toward all flying units.
Military — After action reports: +experience from combat.
Military — Their finest hour: +production towards air units, +strength in home territory.
Military — Martial law: -war weariness, ++loyalty for cities with garrisons.
Military — Defense of the motherland: NO war weariness from combat in home territory, doubel production for support units.
Military — Fortress doctrine: ++city defense for cities with walls.
Military — Siege engineers: +production towards siege units.
Military — Amphibious assault: No combat penalty when attacking from water or across a river.
Military — Mountain warfare: +combat strength in hill and mountain tiles.
Military — Jungle warfare specialists: +combat strength in forest and jungle tiles.
Military — Desert foxes: +combat strength in desert tiles.
Military — Tundra tigers: +combat strength in tundra tiles.
Military — Night ops: +combat strength during night cycles.
Military — Combat medics: Slightly heal every turn even if action is taken.
Military — Deep cover: Reduced visibility to enemy units.
Military — Rapid deployment: Faster airlifting and parachuting of units.
Military — Anti-air defense: ++strength against air units.
Military — Special forces: Guerrilla warfare results in +gold from enemy units defeated.
Military — Field repairs: Damaged units can heal up to 20% in a single turn.
Military — Reinforced armor: +defense against ranged attacks.
Military — Stealth technology: -visibility for enemy recon units.
Military — Biological warfare: Use of disease to reduce enemy city health over time.
Military — Nuclear deterrent: Reduced war weariness from nuclear weapon possession.
Military — Home guard: Civilians in cities gain minimal defense strength.
Military — Tactical nukes: Reduced gold and production cost for small scale nuclear weapons.
Military — Mobilization plans: Faster unit production during war declarations.
Military — Combined arms: +combat strength when adjacent to another unit type.
Military — Decryption: +strength against enemy units using encryption.
Military — Guerilla tactics: +combat strength in enemy territory.
Military — Deep sea operations: +production towards submarines.
Military — War economy: Convert civilian factories to military production during war.
Military — Operational intelligence: Reveal enemy unit movements in fog of war.
Military — Civil defense: Increase civilian resistance during sieges.
Military — Urban warfare: +combat strength in city districts.
Military — Defensive pacts: Reduced war weariness from allied wars.
Military — Remote warfare: +production for drone and remote operated units.
Military — Cyber warfare: Disrupt enemy production and science yield.
Military — Electronic warfare: Disable enemy communication, reducing coordination.
Military — Eldritch pact: +mana regeneration for mages in combat.
Military — Dragon riders: +production towards dragon and wyvern units.
Military — Silvered weapons: ++combat strength against undead and lycanthropes.
Military — Mystic woods: +combat strength for units in enchanted forests.
Military — Runestone enchantments: Fortifications get a defense bonus against magical attacks.
Military — Ranger's guild: Faster movement in forests and jungles.
Military — Dwarven engineering: +production towards siege machines and tunneling units.
Military — Elven archers: ++accuracy for ranged units in woodlands.
Military — Necromantic rites: Can raise fallen enemy units as undead.
Military — Sacred ground: +loyalty and defense for cities built on consecrated land.
Military — Beast taming: +production towards mythical beasts and cavalry.
Military — Underworld connections: Access to demon units in exchange for gold or mana.
Military — Pixie dust: Flying units receive a speed boost.
Military — Druidic circle: Heal units faster in natural terrains like forests and plains.
Military — Witch hunter's edict: ++strength against magic-using units.
Military — Sirens of the sea: Naval units can charm and capture enemy units.
Military — Elemental alignment: Elemental units receive +strength based on terrain (e.g., fire elementals on volcanic tiles).
Military — Paladin's oath: Holy warriors gain +strength against dark magic units.
Military — Goblin sappers: Gain gold when destroying enemy fortifications.
Military — Enchanted forges: Magic-infused weapons provide +combat strength.
Military — Moonlit rituals: Were-units gain additional transformation turns.
Military — Summoner's focus: Reduce mana cost for summoning spells.
Military — Mystic portals: Instantaneous movement between allied cities.
Military — Faerie allies: Gain the support of light magical creatures in forests.
Military — Shadowstep: Units can teleport short distances in combat.
Military — Celestial blessings: Units gain defense bonuses during the day.
Military — Lunar warriors: Units gain strength bonuses during the night.
Military — Frost guard: +combat strength in snowy and icy terrains.
Military — Phoenix rebirth: A fallen unit has a chance to revive once per battle.
Military — Alchemy mastery: Produce special potions to boost unit abilities temporarily.
Military — Eagle-eyed griffon riders: Increase visibility range for aerial units.
Military — Petrified defenses: Cities gain stone golems as defensive units.
Military — Mages' sanctuary: Cities with magic academies resist enemy spells.
Military — Troll regenerators: Troll units heal rapidly every turn.
Military — Crystal resonance: Extract more magic energy from crystal mines.
Economic — God king: +gold and +faith in capital.
Economic — Urban planning: +production in all cities.
Economic — Ilkum: +production to regional development.
Economic — Caravansaries: +gold from all trade routes.
Economic — Corvee: +production toward wonders.
Economic — Land surveyors - reduce cost for expansion.
Economic — Colonization: +Production towards new cities.
Economic — Insulae: +housing in all cities with 2+districts.
Economic — Natural Philosophy: double efficiency to campus-adjacent tiles.
Economic — Scripture: double efficiency to holy site adjacent tiles.
Economic — Naval Infrastructure: double efficiency to harbor adjacent tiles.
Economic — Serfdom: +builder efficiency.
Economic — Meritocracy: +culter per specialty district.
Economic — Trade confederation: +culture and science from cross region trade routes.
Economic — Aesthetics: double efficiency to theater adjacent tiles.
Economic — Medina quarter: +housing to cities with 3+ specialty districts.
Economic — Town Charters: double efficiency to commercial hub adjacent districts.
Economic — Gothic architecture: +production to wonders.
Economic — Civil prestige: + amenity and housing to established govenors.
Economic — Simultaneum: double faith effectivness and even more if adjacent to 3 adjacency bones.
Economic — Religious orders: +faith based combat.
Economic — Triangular trade: ++gold and +faith from all trade routes.
Economic — Rationalism: double science efficiency from campus, +population.
Economic — Free market: double gold efficiency from commercial hub, +population.
Economic — Liberalism: +amenities to all cities with 2+ specialty districts.
Economic — Public works: +production toward regional development and builder efficiency.
Economic — Skyscrapers: +production towards wonders.
Economic — Grand opera: double culture efficiency from theater square, +population.
Economic — Public transport: +gold when farms are replaced with neighborhoods, +food, gold, and production from neighborhoods.
Economic — Expropiation: -cost for regional expansion.
Economic — Market economy: +gold, culture, and science per interegional trade routes + strategic resources.
Economic — Economic union: double commercial hub and harbor district adjaceny bonuses.
Economic — New Deal: ++housing, +amenities ---gold to cities with 3+ specialty districts.
Economic — Five-year-plan: double campus and industrial zone adjacency bonuses.
Economic — Collectivization: ++food and + Production from domestic trade routes .
Economic — Heritage tourism: double tourism from great works and artifacts .
Economic — Sports media: double theater square adjacency bonuses, stadiums +amenity.
Economic — Satellite Broadcasts: quadruple tourism from great works of music .
Economic — Ecommerce: +++production and ++++gold from international trade routes, half from others.
Economic — Arcane commerce: +gold for every magical district in a city.
Economic — Mystic merchants: Increased trade range and +gold for trading with cities containing magic academies.
Economic — Enchanted craftsmanship: +production for items and constructs imbued with magic.
Economic — Ethereal roads: Trade routes are unhindered by natural barriers.
Economic — Alchemy guilds: Convert a fraction of science into gold each turn.
Economic — Fey markets: Nighttime trade routes yield extra gold and enchantments.
Economic — Goblin bartering: Reduce cost of marketplace items and services.
Economic — Dragon hoards: +gold storage capacity and interest on stored gold.
Economic — Witch's bounty: Farms on enchanted lands produce extra food and rare ingredients.
Economic — Underworld deals: +gold and -production cost for deals involving dark magic or forbidden artifacts.
Economic — Dwarven mines: +production in mountain cities and access to rare metals.
Economic — Elven arts: +culture and gold from crafted elven goods.
Economic — Centaur caravans: Increase trade route speed and +gold from overland trade routes.
Economic — Oracle taxation: Predict and collect taxes more efficiently for +gold.
Economic — Mermaid merchants: +gold for coastal and oceanic trade routes.
Economic — Nymph groves: Woods and forests tiles produce extra gold and culture.
Economic — Sphinx riddles: Cultural landmarks yield additional tourism and gold.
Economic — Enchanted tolls: Roads and bridges generate +gold from passing trade routes.
Economic — Pixie fairs: Festivals yield double gold and happiness in cities.
Economic — Troll bridges: Control and tax strategic routes for extra income.
Economic — Golem labor: +production in cities with an alchemy district.
Economic — Elemental forges: Produce weapons and armor imbued with elemental properties.
Economic — Unicorn sanctuaries: Magical beast areas generate gold and faith.
Economic — Phoenix theaters: +culture and tourism from theaters in desert regions.
Economic — Lycanthrope labor: Farms yield extra food during full moons.
Economic — Vampire investments: Long-term investments yield high returns.
Economic — Astral markets: Trade with other realms for rare goods and resources.
Economic — Crystal caverns: Mines in magical areas yield extra gold and gems.
Economic — Necromancer’s coin: Raise spectral miners to extract gold from haunted mines.
Economic — Dimensional bazaar: Access goods from parallel dimensions.
Economic — Coven's contract: Witches increase productivity in exchange for resources.
Economic — Celestial observatories: Enhance science and gold output in cities with an observatory.
Economic — Sorcerer’s patronage: Magic academies yield additional gold and influence.

Wildcard — Navigation: +great admiral development.

Wildcard — Symphonies: ++great musician development, also + per broadcast center.
Wildcard — Military organization: ++great general development and +movement, also + per Armory.
Wildcard — Laissez-Faire: ++great merchant development, also + per Bank, ++great admiral development, also +per shipyard.
Wildcard — Nobel prize: ++great scientist development.
Wildcard — Science Foundations: ++great scientist development, also + per university, ++great engineer development per power plant, also +per factory.
Wildcard — Aerospace contractors: +aluminum and +power.
Wildcard — Space tourism: reduce tourism towards other civilizations.
Wildcard — Hallyu: Improved bards.
Wildcard — Non-state actors: improved spies.
Wildcard — Integrated attack logistics: +movement starting in enemy territory, +Production towards detah robots.
Wildcard — Rabblerousing: +diplomatic favor, -cost of diplomatic actions.
Wildcard — Diplomatic capital: ++diplomatic favor.
Wildcard — Global coalition: +++combat strength in friendly territory.
Wildcard — Autocratic legacy: +Yield for all government buildings.
Wildcard — Republican legacy: +housing and amenities.
Wildcard — Oligarchic legacy: ++combat strength for all land melee, anti-calvary, and naval melee.
Wildcard — Mercantile legacy: +gold for cities with an established govenor.
Wildcard — Monarchic legacy: +housing per level of wall.
Wildcard — Theocratic legacy: ++religious strength and faith .
Wildcard — Communist legacy: +production based on size.
Wildcard — Democratic legacy: +production and housing per district, ++food and Production per suzerain trade route.
Wildcard — Fascist legacy: ++combat strength and -war weariness.
Wildcard — Dragon Diplomacy: Influence points gained with city-states that have dragons.
Wildcard — Eldritch Rites: ++great mage development, also + per arcane tower.
Wildcard — Fae Favor: Forest tiles yield extra culture and enchanted resources.
Wildcard — Necromantic Nexus: Raise undead units faster with +great necromancer development.
Wildcard — Oracle's Insight: Boost to decision-making during diplomatic events.
Wildcard — Elemental Pact: Enhanced yield from elemental resources (fire, water, air, earth).
Wildcard — Dwarven Traditions: ++great smith development, also + per forge.
Wildcard — Elven Legacy: Forest cities gain +culture and +amenities.
Wildcard — Goblin Markets: Black markets yield extra gold and rare items.
Wildcard — Orcish Horde: Increase in combat strength for each adjacent orc unit.
Wildcard — Vampire Covens: +health regeneration for vampiric units.
Wildcard — Lycanthropic Ties: Werewolf units gain +combat strength during full moon.
Wildcard — Pirate Code: Coastal raiding yields extra gold and resources.
Wildcard — Enchanted Alliances: Improved relations with magic-based civilizations.
Wildcard — Centaur Nomads: +movement in open terrains.
Wildcard — Mystic Enclave: Improved arcane research and magic item crafting.
Wildcard — Bardic Circles: Enhanced culture and music in cities with bard guilds.
Wildcard — Fey Court Relations: Gain favor with otherworldly realms.
Wildcard — Celestial Alignment: Temples and observatories yield extra faith and science.
Wildcard — Underworld Deals: Access to dark magic and forbidden resources at a cost.
Wildcard — Giant Clans: Improved strength and production in mountain regions.
Wildcard — Merman Mercenaries: Enhanced naval combat strength in deep waters.
Wildcard — Troll Bridges: Control and tax strategic mountain passes.
Wildcard — Djinn Pacts: Wishes granted at a cost, with a chance of unpredictable outcomes.
Wildcard — Gryphon Riders: Enhanced vision and combat strength in hilly terrains.
Wildcard — Phoenix Academies: Research institutions yield extra science and culture.
Wildcard — Ancient Golems: Enhanced defense in cities with golem workshops.
Wildcard — Dragon's Lore: Access to ancient knowledge and rare resources.
Wildcard — Behemoth Training: Large creatures provide enhanced city defense.
Wildcard — Ethereal Portals: Access to other realms for trade and diplomacy.
Wildcard — Shadow Council: Improved espionage and intelligence gathering.
Diplomatic — Charismatic leader: +influence.
Diplomatic — Diplomatic League: +envoys.
Diplomatic — Paetorium: +loyalty.
Diplomatic — Merchant Confederation: +gold from envoys.
Diplomatic — Colonial offices: +citygrowth in initial region, +loyalty in different regions.
Diplomatic — Machiavellianism: +spies efficiency.
Diplomatic — Wisselbanken: +food, +production, +alliance points from trade routes to cities or city states.
Diplomatic — Colonial Taxes: +gold and production for cities in new regions.
Diplomatic — Raj: +gold, science, culture, faith for suzerain status, and trade routes to those city states.
Diplomatic — Police state: -enemy spy effectiveness, -amenities, -loyalty.
Diplomatic — Nuclear espionage: +spy effectiveness when stealing tech.
Diplomatic — Arsenal of Democracy: ++food and production for both allied cities in a trade route.
Diplomatic — Gunboat Diplomacy: Open borders with city states ++influence toward envoys.
Diplomatic — Cryptography: -enemy spy effectiveness in your lands, +spy effectiveness in enemy lands.
Diplomatic — Containment: +envoy effectiveness if a different government.
Diplomatic — Music Censorship: -bard proliferation from other civs, -1 amenity in big cities.
Diplomatic — International space agency: +science per city state suzerain status and allied civ.
Diplomatic — Collective activism: +culture per city state suzerain and allied civ.
Diplomatic — Communications office: +loyalty per city.
Diplomatic — Divine Mandate: Boosted relations with civilizations following the same religion.
Diplomatic — Siren's Song: Coastal city-states are more influenced by your envoys.
Diplomatic — Emissary Enclave: Earn a free envoy every time a new city is founded.
Diplomatic — Beast Tamer Accords: Boosted relations with civilizations that use beast units.
Diplomatic — Arcane Alignment: Boosted influence with magic-rich civilizations and city-states.
Diplomatic — Heraldic Traditions: Amplified diplomatic effects from sending and receiving delegations.
Diplomatic — Propaganda Bureau: Reduce loyalty drops from external sources.
Diplomatic — Tribal Conclaves: Boosted relations with tribal civilizations and city-states.
Diplomatic — Peacekeeper Force: Increased influence when mediating peace between warring states.
Diplomatic — Druidic Dialogues: Forest city-states provide extra faith when suzerain.
Diplomatic — Silk Road Diplomacy: Trade routes to other civilizations provide extra gold and cultural influence.
Diplomatic — Nomadic Treaties: +food from trade routes with nomadic tribes and civilizations.
Diplomatic — Ethereal Embassies: Establish embassies in other dimensions or realms for unique diplomatic benefits.
Diplomatic — Artifact Accords: City-states gift ancient artifacts periodically when suzerain.
Diplomatic — Dragon Summit: Periodic diplomatic meetings with dragon-owning civilizations yield bonuses.
Diplomatic — Celestial Diplomacy: Improved relations and bonuses from civilizations based in the sky.
Diplomatic — Abyssal Agreements: Enhanced trade and diplomatic bonuses with underwater civilizations.
Diplomatic — Pandemonium Pact: Diplomatic benefits when dealing with infernal or demonic realms.
Diplomatic — Guardian Guilds: Loyalty bonus in cities with mythical beast guardians.
Diplomatic — Harmonious Halls: Amplified cultural exchange boosts with civilizations in peace treaties.
Diplomatic — Elder Councils: Gain unique knowledge and bonuses from elder races when in good diplomatic standing.
Diplomatic — Starborn Treaties: Increased scientific collaboration with civilizations from other planets or galaxies.
Diplomatic — Festival Forums: Diplomatic bonuses during world festivals or events.
Diplomatic — Necromantic Neutrality: Improved relations with undead or necromancer-led states.
Diplomatic — Sanctuary States: Offering refuge to exiles from other civilizations boosts international reputation and influence.
Diplomatic — Mystic Mediations: Enhanced diplomatic results from interactions with magical city-states or civilizations.
Diplomatic — Cultural Consulates: Boost cultural ties and gain influence faster with civilizations sharing similar arts and traditions.
Dark Age — Monasticism: +++science for cities with a holy site, -culture in all cities.
Dark Age — Twilight valor: ++combat strength for all melee, cannot heal outside fo territory.
Dark Age — Inquisition: +++++religious strength, -science in all cities.
Dark Age — Isolationism: ++food and ++production, no regional expansion allowed.
Dark Age — Letters of Marque: doubel production for naval units, ++movement, double trade route plunder yield, half all regular trade route yield.
Dark Age — Robber Barons: +++++gold in cities with stock exchange, ++production for factories, --amenities.
Dark Age — Elite Forces: double experience for combat, ++gold cost for maintenance.
Dark Age — Collectivism: +food and ++housing per farm, double industrial zone adjacency bonuses, --great person development.
Dark Age — Rogue state: +++++production towards covilization weapons, no envoy development.
Dark Age — Flower power: double toruism for civs not at war, double cost for all land units.
Dark Age — Cyber Warfare: +++++combat strength for advanced units, civ grievances do not decay.
Dark Age — Automated workforce: ++production towards city projects, -amenity and ----loyalty in cities.
Dark Age — Disinformation campaign: +++diplomatic favor per broadcast center, -science and culture in all cities.
Dark Age — Decentralization: small cities gain ++loyalty, large cities --gold.
Dark Age — Samderzhaviye: +++++Production in the capital, no governors allowed.
Dark Age — Soft targets: +++++combat strength vs city attacks and defenses, --combat strength vs units.
Dark Age — Despotic paternalism: ++loyalty for cities with governors, --science and culture for cities without governors.
Dark Age — Cult of Desolation: +++faith from shrines and temples, --population growth in all cities.
Dark Age — Plague Doctors: Reduce disease-related population loss, -gold in all cities due to increased costs.
Dark Age — Tribalism: +loyalty in cities without districts, -production toward district development.
Dark Age — Nihilistic Crusades: ++++religious combat strength, no religious conversions allowed.
Dark Age — Warlord Rule: ++++strength for all units in home territory, no diplomatic actions with other civilizations.
Dark Age — Pagan Rites: ++faith from unimproved forest tiles, -culture and science in all cities.
Dark Age — Feudal Fragmentation: ++production and ++gold in cities with a castle, no envoy development.
Dark Age — Distrust of Alchemy: +++production towards military units, no science development from campuses.
Dark Age — Hermit Kingdom: ++++defense against espionage, no international trade routes allowed.
Dark Age — Mystic Obscurantism: +++faith for cities with a wonder, -science in all cities.
Dark Age — Ritual Sacrifices: +loyalty per unit sacrificed, -population growth.
Dark Age — Siege Mentality: ++++city defense strength, no unit production allowed.
Dark Age — Arcane Prohibition: Double gold from trade routes, no magical unit or building production.
Dark Age — Cannibal Convictions: +++food yield from defeated units, --diplomatic favor.
Dark Age — Guild Monopolies: +++gold from commercial hubs, no new trade routes allowed.
Dark Age — Witch Hunts: +loyalty from executing units, --culture and --science.
Dark Age — Barbaric Traditions: +++strength for all non-ranged units, no cultural development from theaters.
Dark Age — Crumbling Infrastructure: +gold from disbanding buildings, -amenities and --production.
Dark Age — Xenophobic Zeal: +++combat strength vs foreign civilizations, -trade yield and -diplomatic favor.
Dark Age — Mortal Dread: Increase city defense strength by sacrificing population, no growth in that city for a number of turns.
Dark Age — Black Market Pacts: Double gold from city states, no influence or envoy growth.
Dark Age — Lost Legends: Retrieve powerful, one-time use items, --culture and --faith from all sources.
Dark Age — Ancestral Devotion: All monuments yield +++faith, no new cultural policies can be adopted.
Dark Age — Militant Theocracy: Religious units can attack and defend, no science gain from campus districts.
Dark Age — Cursed Lands: +loyalty for cities with no improvements, -gold and -food production.
Dark Age — Treacherous Seas: Naval units gain +++strength, no international naval trade routes allowed.
Dark Age — Paranoid Fortress: +++city defenses against sieges, cannot build or upgrade units.
Dark Age — Arcane Isolation: +magic unit strength, all non-magical units have decreased strength.
Dark Age — Decadent Aristocracy: +++gold from luxury resources, --production in all cities.
Dark Age — Soulbound Soldiers: Recruit units at no production cost, but sacrifice population, -loyalty for each unit recruited this way.
Dark Age — Dark Rituals: +++faith and ++great prophet development, --population growth in all cities.
Dark Age — Cryptic Cults: Gain secret societies' support, -relations with all other civilizations.
Dark Age — Forlorn Frontiers: +++strength against barbarians, -strength against other civilizations.
Dark Age — Lost Crafts: +++production for wonders, no new districts can be built.
Dark Age — Blighted Harvests: +++gold and ++faith from farms, but --food.
Dark Age — Desolate Diplomacy: +++spy effectiveness, no peace deals or alliances can be made.
Dark Age — Cursed Commodities: +++gold from mines, no production from mines.
Dark Age — Toxic Traditions: Increased combat strength when fighting in home territory, no growth or development in culture.
Dark Age — Unholy Alliances: Forge strong bonds with barbarians, making them allies, -relations with all city-states.
Dark Age — Pandemic Panacea: Population does not decrease due to disease, but --science and -culture in all cities.
Dark Age — Veil of Obscurity: All cities are hidden from other civilizations, no international trade or diplomacy allowed.
Dark Age — Eldritch Energies: Gain access to powerful dark spells, other civilizations become more hostile.
Dark Age — Cataclysmic Cultivation: Terrain yields are doubled, but natural disasters occur twice as often.
Golden Age — Monumentality: Faster regional expansion, +faith based expansion.
Golden Age — Exodus of the evangelists: Faster religious spread, more effective prosletizing, ++great prophet development.
Golden Age — Mandala state: ++culture for every wonder, --enemy city loyalty per wonder.
Golden Age — Free Inquiry: commercial hub and harbor gold provides science as well, campus provides gold equal to science.
Golden Age — Praetorian guard: ++unit healing per turn, ++production to military buildings.
Golden Age — Sakdina: +loyalty, +all great person development.
Golden Age — Reform the coinage: Traders cannot be plundered, ++gold per specialty district in the foreign city from trades.
Golden Age — Heartbeat of steam: +production toward wonders, science district adjacency bonus also adds production.
Golden Age — To Arms!: +++combat experience, ++Production towards military units.
Golden Age — Culture Industry: +culture per specialty district, ++production towards non-specialty districts, faith based growht and development.
Golden Age — Wish you were here: double tourism for national parks: +++tourism for world wonders.
Golden Age — Popular Front: double diplomatic favor from suzerain status, ++to all trade route yields.
Golden Age — Solidarity: ++gold, faith, and loyalty from govenors.
Golden Age — Sky and stars: +++ production towards flight-based buildings, +science and production in cities with flight based buildings.
Golden Age — Military-industrial complex: +aluminum, oil, and uranium yield, ++combat strength and +combat range.
Golden Age — Elysian Fields: Cities with luxury resources gain ++amenities and ++culture.
Golden Age — Gilded Galley: ++production towards naval units, naval trade routes generate +++gold.
Golden Age — Edicts of Enlightenment: Universities and libraries provide +faith, holy sites grant ++science.
Golden Age — Harmonious Houses: Housing bonuses doubled, cities with max housing gain ++culture and +++food.
Golden Age — Artisan Alliance: Great artists, writers, and musicians are produced 50% faster, their works yield additional +++gold.
Golden Age — Celestial Navigation: Astronomy provides bonus faith, +movement for naval units.
Golden Age — Pinnacle of Prosperity: Cities with a bank or stock exchange gain +culture, +science, and +faith.
Golden Age — Ingenious Infrastructure: Roads built twice as fast, ++gold and +culture from internal trade routes.
Golden Age — Theatre of Triumph: Amphitheaters provide +loyalty, cities with a theater square gain ++population growth.
Golden Age — Masters of Metal: Mines produce +culture, blacksmiths and workshops add +gold to their yields.
Golden Age — Acropolis Ascendance: +loyalty for cities with wonders, wonders are constructed 25% faster.
Golden Age — Garden of Growth: Cities with a Hanging Garden or any park gain ++food and +culture.
Golden Age — Festival of Fertility: +population growth, cities celebrating gain additional +++gold and ++culture.
Golden Age — Beacons of Knowledge: Lighthouses and harbors yield bonus science, naval units gain +experience.
Golden Age — Sovereign Sanctuaries: National Parks yield ++faith and +tourism, natural wonders grant bonus culture to adjacent tiles.
Golden Age — Pillars of Prosperity: Every district adds to city defense strength, ++gold from city centers.
Golden Age — Sage's Symposium: Universities add bonus great person points for great scientists, +science for each adjacent mountain.
Golden Age — Golden Guilds: ++gold for every market and bank, traders also provide a small boost to production.
Golden Age — Chariots of Champions: Calvary units gain +movement and ++combat strength.
Golden Age — Divine Dynasty: +loyalty in cities following your main religion, temples and shrines yield ++gold.
Golden Age — Luminous Liturgy: Religious units move +1 faster and religious combat is more effective. Cities with your religion gain +culture.
Golden Age — Oracle's Oversight: Every oracle provides +science and +culture, enhancing prediction and growth.
Golden Age — Palace Prosperity: Capital city yields ++gold, +science, and +culture for each established governor.
Golden Age — Merchant Magnates: Double bonuses from luxury resources, and they contribute +faith.
Golden Age — Glittering Garrison: Cities with walls produce +culture and +tourism, boosting defense and international appeal.
Golden Age — Visionary Vistas: Cities with breathtaking appeal gain ++faith and +science, inspiring advancements and spiritual growth.
Golden Age — Magnificent Museums: Artifacts and relics yield additional +culture and +tourism, amplifying historical significance.
Golden Age — Golden Galleons: Naval units provide +loyalty when stationed in foreign waters, symbolizing dominance and influence.
Golden Age — Sanctum of Scholars: Every campus district generates +gold and +faith, representing wealth in knowledge and spirituality.
Golden Age — Majestic Monuments: Monuments provide ++culture and boost loyalty, reflecting national pride.
Golden Age — Radiant Railroads: Faster movement on railroads and ++gold from trade routes passing through them.
Golden Age — Pristine Parks: Natural preserves boost city growth and +amenities, emphasizing ecological balance.
Golden Age — Sovereign Seas: Fishing boats provide +gold and +culture, reflecting maritime prosperity.
Golden Age — Architect's Aspiration: Building construction sped up by 25%, cities with wonders gain +production.
Golden Age — Golden Gastronomy: +food from each farm, plantations also yield +culture, celebrating agricultural abundance.
Golden Age — Sacred Summits: Mountains yield +faith, and adjacent tiles gain +production, tapping into the terrain's spiritual and material essence.
Golden Age — Enlightened Estates: Cities with a governor gain +science and +culture, benefiting from administrative expertise.
Golden Age — Dazzling Diplomacy: +influence per turn, and double the rewards from fulfilled emergency and scored competitions.
Golden Age — Glorious Guilds: Great people are acquired 10% faster, reflecting a society at the peak of its cultural and intellectual prowess.
Golden Age — Templar Tradition: Holy sites provide bonus gold and culture, interweaving spiritual and worldly achievements.
Scientific — Academic Assembly: ++science in cities with a campus, +culture in cities with a library.
Scientific — Educational Edicts: Reduce cost and time to build schools and universities by 20%.
Scientific — Research Grants: +gold and +science from trade routes to and from cities with a university.
Scientific — Pedagogical Pioneering: Increase the output of great scientist points by 15%.
Scientific — Lecture Series: +culture and +science in cities with a university and theater square adjacency.
Scientific — Apprenticeship System: +production in cities with a workshop, ++science in cities with an industrial zone.
Scientific — Scholarly Summits: International trade routes provide +science and +gold when trading with civilizations that have more techs/civics.
Scientific — Youth Engagement: Reduce citizen growth needed for new educational buildings, +amenities in cities with a campus.
Scientific — Continued Learning: 10% science bonus for each city with a fully filled campus district.
Scientific — Open Curriculum: Increase loyalty in cities with a campus, +science for each cultural building.
Scientific — Science Symposiums: Gain a research boost each time a scientific building or wonder is completed.
Scientific — Field Studies: +tourism from science buildings and wonders, +science from natural wonders.
Scientific — Laboratory Logistics: Decrease maintenance cost for science buildings by 20%.
Scientific — Education Equality: +loyalty and +amenities in cities that have the same number of educational buildings as the capital.
Scientific — Curiosity Campaign: +science and +culture for each unique luxury resource within city limits.
Scientific — Scientific Sanctions: +combat strength against civilizations that are more technologically advanced.
Scientific — Mentorship Mandates: Faster great person development in cities with universities.
Scientific — Learning Through Leisure: +science from entertainment complexes and water parks.
Scientific — Research Residency: +science in cities with an established governor, ++science if the governor has the 'Scientist' title.
Scientific — Archival Access: Each ancient artifact in the city adds +science and +culture.
Scientific — Classroom Creativity: +culture in cities with a university, further boosted if they have an art museum.
Scientific — Technical Tutorials: Gain a small burst of science each time a unit is trained in cities with a campus.
Scientific — Literary Labs: Gain science for each great work of writing in the city.
Scientific — Innovative Infrastructure: +science for each city building built, and a bonus if adjacent to a campus.
Scientific — Scientific Showcases: World wonders provide +science and +culture, with a larger boost if they are science-related wonders.

Scientific — Eldritch Studies: Gain faith from campuses. Cities with a university have a small chance each turn to gain a free random promotion for a unit, representing the unlocking of arcane secrets.
Scientific — Druidic Pedagogy: Forests adjacent to campuses provide additional science and culture.
Scientific — Gnomish Innovation: +production and +science in cities with mines and quarries. Unlocks special 'Tinkering Workshop' city project that can yield bonuses or rare resources.
Scientific — Mystic Mentorships: Wizards and mage units provide loyalty to the city they are stationed in. Universities increase the power of summoned creatures.
Scientific — Dragonlore Discourses: +science for each mountain tile adjacent to a campus. Enables recruitment of Dragon Scholars, specialized units that can speed up research or defend campuses.
Scientific — Merfolk Marine Biology: Coastal cities with a campus gain extra science. Allows the creation of underwater research labs that further boost ocean resource yields.
Scientific — Celestial Curriculum: Night time yields a small science bonus in cities with an observatory. Chance to receive visions that offer hints on undiscovered techs.
Scientific — Alchemy Academics: Convert a percentage of gold into science each turn. Unlocks the Alchemist unit that can transmute resources.
Scientific — Spectral Scholarship: Haunted cities or those with graveyards receive a culture and science boost. Occasionally spawns friendly ghost units that can perform unique tasks.
Scientific — Faerie Folklore: Ancient groves and magical springs near a campus provide culture and science. Faerie units can be summoned to enhance city districts temporarily.
Scientific — Golem Graduates: Craft golem units in cities with universities. These units can assist in construction, defense, or even scholarly pursuits.
Scientific — Astrological Advancements: Cities with a Stonehenge-like structure gain bonus science. Periodically predict celestial events that grant temporary empire-wide bonuses.
Scientific — Elemental Engineering: Harness the powers of Earth, Air, Fire, and Water. Cities with Elemental Temples gain a diversified bonus depending on the dominant element.
Scientific — Ley Line Learning: Detect magical ley lines on the map, which when harnessed by campuses can provide powerful surges of science and faith.
Scientific — Necronomical Knowledge: Delve into forbidden knowledge. Gain huge science boosts at the risk of spawning hostile undead or causing city unrest.
Scientific — Crystal Cavern Classrooms: Cities built near crystal caverns gain a unique research facility, boosting science and allowing the creation of magical artifacts.
Scientific — Enchanted Education: Libraries enchanted by elven magic yield double science and occasionally produce free great works of writing.
Scientific — Potion Professorship: Researching alchemical potions grants small boosts to units and can produce one-time use items with powerful effects.
Scientific — Summoning Seminars: Learn to summon mythical creatures that can aid in various aspects of civilization from defense to trade.
Scientific — Runic Research: Decipher ancient runes for science bonuses. Runestones can be erected to provide regional effects based on their inscriptions.
Scientific — Portal Physics: Discover interdimensional portals. Allows trade and exchange of knowledge with other realms, granting periodic boosts to random technologies.
Scientific — Chimeric Curriculum: Study the science of combining creatures. Unlock specialized units made up of multiple entities, each with unique abilities beneficial for exploration and defense.
Scientific — Mystical Meteorology: Harness the power of weather magic. Cities with Wizard Towers can influence local climates, benefiting agriculture and staving off natural disasters.
Scientific — Astral Archaeology: Explore ancient celestial ruins. Expeditions can uncover powerful artifacts and technologies from fallen star civilizations.
Scientific — Bardic Biology: Songs and tales unlock secrets of nature. Libraries occasionally produce cultural performances that can inspire technological eurekas or grant bonus science.
Scientific — Temporal Theorems: Delve into the mysteries of time manipulation. Allows limited "rewinding" of certain decisions or speeds up construction and research for short bursts.
Scientific — Goblin Green-tech: Learn eco-friendly innovations from goblins. Improves city sanitation, reduces pollution, and increases yields from recycling centers.
Scientific — Celestial Cartography: Map the stars with celestial beings. Naval and aerial units gain significant bonuses to exploration and movement.
Scientific — Oracle Oversight: Prophecies guide research directions. Random predictions give clues on which technologies or policies might offer extraordinary benefits in the near future.
Scientific — Dreamweaver Dynamics: Tap into the collective dreams of your citizens. Gain science during peaceful times and unlock unique dream-inspired inventions and wonders.
Scientific — Psionic Psychology: Unlock the mental potential of your populace. Enhances productivity, innovation, and defense against espionage in cities with Psionic Schools.
Scientific — Troll Traditional Medicine: Incorporate troll healing techniques. Boosts health in cities, speeds up unit healing, and provides science from forest tiles.
Scientific — Djinn Data Dynamics: Enlist genies in computational tasks. Universities with a Djinn Advisor yield a chance of instantly completing research or city projects.
Scientific — Fey Forest Forestry: Learn sustainable wood harvesting from forest spirits. Increases lumberyard yields and provides a science bonus in cities adjacent to woods.
Scientific — Ethereal Energy Engineering: Harness energy from ethereal realms. Power plants in cities gain a significant boost in production, and excess power can be converted to science.
Justice — Judicious Juries: City courts yield +gold and +culture. Fair trials promote economic and cultural growth.
Justice — Guardian's Grasp: Cities with established governors experience reduced crime, increasing loyalty.
Justice — Warden's Watch: Military units stationed in cities boost +loyalty and +culture, reflecting order and stability.
Justice — Benevolent Barristers: Each diplomatic agreement or alliance provides +science and +culture, symbolizing the power of peaceful negotiations.
Justice — Penitent Prisons: Reduced rebellion and increased +faith in cities with an encampment, emphasizing rehabilitation over punishment.
Justice — Emissary's Equity: Diplomatic units move +1 faster and provide +culture when in foreign lands, promoting fair and balanced relations.
Justice — Ruler's Right: Cities with a monument gain +loyalty and +gold, showing the benefits of just governance.
Justice — Balance Beam: Balanced economy leads to +gold and +culture, representing fair trade and commerce.
Justice — Paladin's Promise: Holy sites yield bonus +loyalty and +gold in cities with your religion, intertwining spirituality and justice.
Justice — Vigilant Vigil: Cities experience reduced spy effectiveness and yield +culture, a testament to watchful guardianship.
Justice — Noble Negotiations: +faith and +gold from successful trade deals, showcasing the power of honorable diplomacy.
Justice — Sanctuary of the Scales: Every courthouse provides +faith and +gold, representing the divine nature of justice.
Justice — Keeper's Code: Cities with walls have increased loyalty and provide +culture, reflecting fortified principles of fairness.
Justice — Diplomat's Decree: Enhanced diplomatic favor for peaceful resolutions, boosting international appeal.
Justice — Equitable Estates: Cities with a governor reduce grievances and provide +gold, emphasizing just rule.
Justice — Legislative Lore: Libraries and universities yield +culture and +loyalty, as knowledge fosters understanding and fairness.
Justice — Gavel's Glory: Cities with a wonder gain +loyalty and +faith, symbolizing the monumental impact of just rule.
Justice — Peacemaker's Pledge: Reduced war weariness and increased +culture in cities not at war, showcasing the benefits of peaceful governance.
Justice — Sentinel's Stand: Forts and encampments provide increased loyalty and +culture, as strategic defense upholds justice.
Justice — Covenant of the Court: Great people provide bonus loyalty in the city they are activated, highlighting the influence of notable figures in promoting justice.
Justice — Defender's Doctrine: Defensive buildings and structures yield +gold, signifying the economic benefits of a just and secure society.
Justice — Harmonious Hearings: For every successful dispute resolution, gain bonus +faith and +culture, fostering a spirit of reconciliation.
Justice — Righteous Regulations: Cities with commercial hubs generate +loyalty, illustrating the trust the populace places in a just economy.
Justice — Wise Whistleblowers: Spies stationed in your own cities yield +culture and +science, reflecting the societal advancements from internal oversight.
Justice — Treaty's Triumph: For every peaceful agreement with other civilizations, gain +gold and +loyalty, valuing diplomatic resolutions.
Justice — Lorekeeper's Litigation: Campuses and theater squares provide bonus +culture, emphasizing the interplay between knowledge and justice.
Justice — Guarded Gates: Cities with walls gain increased trade route yield, showcasing the economic growth from secure and fair cities.
Justice — Mediator's Mantle: Diplomatic units provide +loyalty when stationed in city-states, symbolizing the peacekeeping role of diplomacy.
Justice — Champion's Charter: Military units gain a combat boost when fighting to liberate cities, reflecting the valor of fighting for justice.
Justice — Truthseeker's Tenet: Religious units spread religion more effectively in cities where justice policies are implemented, intertwining justice with faith.
Justice — Impartial Institutions: Government plaza buildings yield +culture and +science, highlighting the convergence of governance and knowledge.
Justice — Sanctified Sanctions: Trade penalties for nations with differing justice systems; however, trading with like-minded civilizations yields extra +gold and +culture.
Justice — Scales of Serenity: Cities with high happiness produce bonus +faith, reflecting the spiritual contentment of a just society.
Justice — Civic Custodians: Every library and university boosts city loyalty, showcasing the importance of educated justice.
Justice — Bastions of Benevolence: For every enemy spy captured, gain +culture and +faith, recognizing the divine significance of thwarting deception.
Justice — Gavel's Grace: Faster courthouse construction and boosted loyalty in recently acquired cities, emphasizing the rapid establishment of justice.
Justice — Jury's Jubilation: Entertainment complexes provide +culture, showing the societal contentment from fair adjudication.
Justice — Restorative Residences: Amenities bonus for cities recovering from war or rebellion, illustrating the healing power of justice.
Justice — Penal Protectors: Prisons generate +gold and increase spy efficiency, emphasizing the dual purpose of correction and safeguarding.
Justice — Emissary Ethics: Envoys in city-states generate +faith, valuing the spiritual importance of just diplomatic conduct.
Justice — Innocence Incentives: Less grievances generated, and faster decay of existing ones, rewarding a civilization for just actions.
Justice — Moral Mariners: Naval units gain combat strength when combating piracy or privateers, exemplifying maritime justice.
Justice — Aegis of Advocacy: Cities with governors receive bonus protection against espionage, valuing the role of local leadership in upholding justice.
Justice — Freedoms' Fortresses: Cities that resist loyalty pressure from autocratic governments gain bonus +production and +culture.
Justice — Edict of Equity: Cities with diverse populations gain bonus +gold and +culture, celebrating the merits of equality.
Justice — Sentinel's Sanctuary: Increased fortification strength for cities, symbolizing the stalwart defense of justice.
Justice — Legislator's Luminance: Science gained from successfully passing or resisting international resolutions, showcasing the blend of diplomacy and knowledge.
Justice — Decree of Dignity: Amenities bonus for cities without slavery or forced labor, honoring the value of freedom.
Justice — Martyr's Memorial: Gain faith and culture from units lost in defense of homeland or liberating cities, cherishing the sacrifice for justice.
Justice — Advocate's Ascendancy: +Science and +culture for every friendly trade route, promoting and valuing international collaboration based on justice principles.
Infrastructure — Paved Pathways: Roads improve movement speed by an additional +1, stimulating faster inter-city connectivity.
Infrastructure — Mighty Masonry: Faster construction of city walls and defenses, ensuring cities are well protected and organized.
Infrastructure — Aqueduct Aesthetics: Aqueducts yield additional +culture and +faith, celebrating the harmonious blend of functionality and art.
Infrastructure — Skyward Scaffolds: All air units get +1 range and cities with airports produce +culture, reflecting the marvel of aviation.
Infrastructure — Stationary Sentinels: Train stations increase trade route yields and enhance city loyalty, centralizing economic growth.
Infrastructure — Warehouse Wonders: City storage capacity increased, allowing for greater accumulation of resources.
Infrastructure — Tunneling Triumphs: Mountain tunnels constructed faster, boosting +production in adjacent tiles.
Infrastructure — Canal Capitals: Cities connected by canals generate bonus +gold and +culture, emphasizing the trade and cultural significance of water routes.
Infrastructure — Grid Greatness: Power plants increase city area effect range and also provide bonus +science.
Infrastructure — Lustrous Landscapes: Districts built on breathtaking appeal tiles receive bonus +production and +gold.
Infrastructure — Subway Superiority: Underground metros offer +culture and speed up internal city movement.
Infrastructure — Tower Traditions: Skyscrapers boost city tourism and generate +gold, showcasing the marvels of modern construction.
Infrastructure — Bridge Builders: Bridges built over rivers boost trade route yields passing through and provide +culture.
Infrastructure — Harbor Havens: Harbors provide increased loyalty and naval units constructed there start with bonus experience.
Infrastructure — Artisan Aquifers: Water mills and dams yield bonus +food and +culture, valuing the blend of functionality and tradition.
Infrastructure — Futuristic Foundations: Smart cities provide +science and use less power, heralding an era of eco-friendly advancements.
Infrastructure — Regal Roads: Roads provide +gold for each city they connect, establishing critical trade arteries.
Infrastructure — Digital Districts: City districts equipped with digital infrastructure boost +science and +culture.
Infrastructure — Eco-Enclaves: Eco-parks and green zones enhance city growth and +amenities, emphasizing urban sustainability.
Infrastructure — Monolithic Monorails: Monorails increase city tourism and offer bonus +gold for every domestic trade route using them.
Infrastructure — Druidic Drains: Sewer systems provide +faith and enhance city growth, invoking the blessings of nature spirits.
Infrastructure — Wyvern Waypoints: Skyports for mythical creatures offer bonus tourism and +culture, showcasing the blend of magic and architecture.
Infrastructure — Enchanted Expressways: Roads shimmer with magic, boosting trade route yields and reducing movement costs for magical units.
Infrastructure — Mystical Mines: Mines yield additional +faith and +gold, believed to tap into the very veins of the Earth's magic.
Infrastructure — Crystal Canals: Waterways glitter with enchanted waters, boosting health in adjacent cities and producing +culture.
Infrastructure — Golem Guarded Galleries: Tunnels protected by stone golems increase trade route safety and yield bonus +production.
Infrastructure — Faery Fountains: Aqueducts are accompanied by faery pools, providing additional +faith and +amenities.
Infrastructure — Sorcerer's Subways: Underground routes are faster and magically shielded, offering bonus +science and protection against espionage.
Infrastructure — Celestial Ceilings: Starlight-enhanced rooftops provide bonus +faith and +science during nighttime, drawing power from constellations.
Infrastructure — Portals Plaza: Magic portals reduce inter-city movement time, bolstering +culture and connection with other realms.
Infrastructure — Elemental Elevators: Rise to great heights or delve deep below using elemental forces, boosting city +production and +culture.
Infrastructure — Pixie-powered Powerhouses: Power plants run by pixie dust generate more power and provide bonus +faith.
Infrastructure — Enchanted Embankments: River banks enchanted to bless passing vessels, boosting trade route yields and city defense.
Infrastructure — Ley Line Lanes: Roads built over ley lines offer +science and +faith bonuses, harnessing the planet's mystical energy.
Infrastructure — Arcane Arteries: Magical conduits enhance district adjacency bonuses and provide additional +mana (or equivalent magical resource).
Infrastructure — Sylph Silos: Storage facilities guarded by wind spirits ensure better resource preservation and generate bonus +culture.
Infrastructure — Witchlight Wharfs: Harbors illuminated by ethereal lights boost naval unit production and generate +tourism.
Infrastructure — Astral Avenues: Roads touched by star magic enhance the loyalty of connected cities.
Infrastructure — Dwarven Depths: Mines delve deeper and are more productive, yielding additional +gold and +production, guarded by sturdy dwarves.
Infrastructure — Phoenix Perches: Elevated platforms allow the mythical birds to roost, providing +faith and enhanced city growth from their blessings.
Infrastructure — Sentinel Statues: Stone statues come alive to protect cities, increasing city defense and boosting +culture.
Infrastructure — Ethereal Bridges: Bridges made of pure energy, enhancing trade and unit movement across water.
Infrastructure — Mystic Mills: Powered by elemental winds, increasing +food and +production in cities.
Infrastructure — Forge of the Ancients: Blacksmiths harness forgotten techniques, boosting production of siege and melee units.
Infrastructure — Lunar Lanterns: Streetlights empowered by moon magic, increasing +tourism and city appeal during nighttime.
Infrastructure — Merfolk Markets: Underwater trade centers, boosting sea trade routes and offering +gold.
Infrastructure — Guardian Groves: Sacred trees shield and nurture cities, offering +amenities and +faith.
Infrastructure — Elemental Energy Grid: Draws power from earth, air, fire, and water, bolstering power output and granting +science.
Infrastructure — Arcane Aquifers: Magically enriched water sources, providing health benefits and bonus +faith to cities.
Infrastructure — Nymph Nurtured Nurseries: Plantations and farms blessed by nymphs yield additional +food and +culture.
Infrastructure — Dragon Drafted Dams: Dams reinforced and overseen by dragons, ensuring optimal water control and generating +faith.
Infrastructure — Veil Vaults: Warehouses shielded in magic veils, preventing theft and boosting city +gold.
Infrastructure — Elven Elevations: Tree platforms built by elves, enhancing wood resource yields and providing +culture.
Infrastructure — Spectral Stations: Ghostly transportation hubs, reducing movement cost and boosting trade route safety.
Infrastructure — Giant's Groundworks: Vast construction projects overseen by giants, speeding up district construction.
Infrastructure — Wizard Watchtowers: Provides enhanced visibility and defense, also boosts +science from arcane research.
Infrastructure — Chimera Caravans: Mystical creatures assisting in trade, doubling trade route yields.
Infrastructure — Blessed Bazaars: Marketplaces imbued with good fortune, increasing +gold and +culture.
Infrastructure — Mystic Moats: Moats filled with magical waters, bolstering city defense and deterring enemy spies.
Infrastructure — Rune Railways: Trains powered by ancient runes, enhancing connection between cities and offering +production bonuses.
Agriculture — Harvest Heralds: Farmers summon minor elementals to aid them, increasing +food yield from farms.
Agriculture — Mystical Mycelium: Fungi networks interwoven with mana, boosting food production in forests.
Agriculture — Celestial Crops: Farms planted under zodiac signs, yielding bonus +faith and +food during respective seasons.
Agriculture — Druidic Design: Circle formations guided by druids, ensuring maximal growth and +culture from plantations.
Agriculture — Waters of Wishing: Holy wells and springs bless the land, increasing +food and +faith in adjacent tiles.
Agriculture — Centaur Tillers: Centaur tribes aid in tilling, speeding up farm establishment and increasing +production.
Agriculture — Phoenix Poultry: Eternal birds that regenerate, supplying endless +food in the city they reside in.
Agriculture — Treant Orchards: Living trees offering fruits, yielding bonus +food and +wood.
Agriculture — Nymph Nourishment: Mystic waters increasing the fertility of adjacent lands, offering bonus +food and +culture.
Agriculture — Alchemy Acres: Transmutation of common elements into rich soil, enhancing all crop yields.
Agriculture — Faerie Fertilizers: Enchanted compost made by faeries, doubling farm outputs.
Agriculture — Moonlit Meadows: Grasslands bathed in moonbeams, producing bonus +food during night cycles.
Agriculture — Blessed Beehives: Beehives overseen by minor deities, producing ambrosial honey boosting +food and +faith.
Agriculture — Elemental Fisheries: Fishing spots imbued with elemental essence, multiplying fish yield and granting bonus +science.
Agriculture — Star-Seed Sowing: Planting guided by celestial patterns, predicting best growth periods for bonus +food.
Agriculture — Witch's Greenhouses: Enchanted greenhouses, extending growth seasons and warding off pests.
Agriculture — Spiritual Silos: Granaries guided by ancestral spirits, preserving more food and providing +faith.
Agriculture — Dew of Dawn: Magical dew appearing at dawn, rejuvenating crops for faster growth.
Agriculture — Pegasus Pastures: Flying herds grazing in the skies, providing special resources.
Agriculture — Giant Grain Silos: Massive storage overseen by giants, increasing city food stockpile and reducing food consumption.
Agriculture — Vineyard Valkyries: Blessed vineyards where Valkyries visit, producing wines that grant +culture and +faith.
Agriculture — Enchanted Ecosystem: Magical animals and plants coexist, accelerating mutual growth and enhancing yields.
Agriculture — Zephyr's Zeal: Wind spirits aid in spreading pollen, ensuring every crop is pollinated, boosting +food.
Agriculture — Merfolk Markets: Trade agreements with merfolk societies, bringing deep-sea delicacies and increasing +gold and +food.
Agriculture — Golem Growers: Animated clay workers tirelessly tending to farms, enhancing productivity and +food yield.
Agriculture — Dragonfruit Orchards: Mythical plants cultivated under dragon's breath, yielding fruits that grant +science and +culture.
Agriculture — Midas Meadows: Golden fields where every grain is precious, doubling +gold income from farms.
Agriculture — Hydra-Hydroponics: Multi-sprouted plants cultivated in magical waters, multiplying crop output.
Agriculture — Rune-Rooted Groves: Trees inscribed with ancient runes, their fruits imbue knowledge (+science) and sustenance (+food).
Agriculture — Lunar Lakes: Waters influenced by moon cycles, providing rare aquatic plants and fish for enhanced +food.
Agriculture — Pixie Pastures: Lands where ethereal creatures aid in nurturing livestock, increasing +food and +faith.
Agriculture — Harmony Hedges: Plants grown in musical patterns, producing harmonious vibrations that boost +culture and +food.
Agriculture — Sprite Sprinklers: Little spirits that bless and water the land, ensuring plentiful harvests.
Agriculture — Nectar of Nemeton: Sacred groves producing divine nectar, amplifying +faith and +food from plantations.
Agriculture — Will-o'-Wisp Wetlands: Marshes lit by ethereal lights, attracting rare creatures and plants for +food and +alchemy.
Agriculture — Sylph Silage: Magical fodder consumed by animals, increasing livestock yield and quality.
Agriculture — Elysian Estates: Farms said to mimic those in the afterlife, producing otherworldly yields and +culture.
Agriculture — Sorcerer's Soil: Earth infused with arcane energies, making crops resistant to diseases and pests.
Agriculture — Ley Line Landscapes: Farms built on magical ley lines, exponentially boosting growth and energy (+science).
Agriculture — Cherub's Crop Circles: Fields visited by celestial beings, creating patterns that enhance growth and grant +faith.
Agriculture — Phoenix Fertilizer: Ashes from the legendary bird enrich the soil, granting rebirth and vigor to crops.
Agriculture — Basilisk Berries: Rare fruits that petrify pests, ensuring that crops remain untouched and yield maximum +food.
Agriculture — Elemental Earth: Soil interwoven with Earth elementals, strengthening crops and increasing resistance to natural disasters.
Agriculture — Mana Meadows: Fields suffused with raw magical energy, ensuring abundant harvests that boost +faith and +magic.
Agriculture — Nymph Nurseries: Gardens tended by water spirits, enhancing growth and the nutritional value of produce.
Agriculture — Troll Tillage: Hardworking trolls turn the earth, aerating the land and allowing for more potent growth.

Agriculture — Nectar Nimbus: Clouds that rain down divine nectar, rejuvenating the lands below and granting +faith.
Agriculture — Griffin Grain Lofts: Aerial granaries overseen by griffins, ensuring food safety and increasing +food storage.

Urban Design — Sorcerer Suburbs: Residential areas favored by mages, enhancing +magic research and +education.
